next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 11

Three days after they left Thessa's settlement, they found the bridge. Kem's shoulders still remembered every borrowed bit of Strength he'd used to drag the pack up bad hills, and Thessa's question had only gotten meaner with distance. What remained of the bridge, gray timbers slumped into the current and iron bolts orange with rust, suggested years of neglect rather than sudden collapse. The southern end had rotted away entirely. Northern pilings stood like teeth in a broken jaw, with water rushing between them in brown surges that carried sticks and clods of soil.

At the bank, Kem watched the river. It was wider than he'd expected, twenty meters at least, loud with the kind of sound water made when it was working rather than resting. The surface moved fast and uneven, cresting over submerged rocks, carrying silt from somewhere upstream where it had rained recently. During the three days south from Thessa's place, the terrain had turned barer and less maintained. Fences had thinned. Cultivated ground had given way to rougher scrub. The road itself had narrowed from packed earth to a track that was mostly suggestion. This was the boundary between the Spent lands and whatever lay beyond them.

"How deep?" he asked.

Vel was crouching at the bank, testing the current with a stripped branch. She pulled it out and studied the wet line. "It'll be waist-deep at the edges. Chest-high in the middle, maybe higher. Rain upstream pushed it up."

"So we spend."

"No."

"Bridge Conjuration would take three levels. I could span it in twenty seconds."

"No."

"I've got Wate...
open_hooks_to_carry: Vel and Thessa's history: partners who split over the question of spending
Thessa's Hoarded deal — economic security for route information
The Hoarded representative visibly affected by early-stage Stillness: speech slowing mid-sentence, eyes going intermittently vacant — Stillness rendered in present tense outside the Vault
Thessa reveals Vel came through years ago asking about high-level defectors — she has been looking for a Kem for a long time
Vel consults Maren's route journal to cross-reference Thessa's partial information; Thessa recognizes it
Kem burns 18 levels on defensive abilities during the escape from the Hoarded representative
The unspoken confrontation: Vel chose spending over people who loved her

payoffs_to_land.description: Maren's route journal is used for navigation and recognized by Thessa — the object gains emotional weight as a link to Vel's past
Stillness encountered in present tense outside the Vault — the Hoarded representative's deterioration gives the reader direct, non-backstory evidence of the system's cost

prev_tail_excerpt:
...e instead of burning a level on command. Dren's arm was closed because Kem had done clumsy work and kept doing it until it held. A life without the Ledger sounded brutal. It also sounded clean in a way the Vault never had.

"I'm not sure if that sounds better or worse," he said.

"It depends on the day," Vel said.

They let the conversation stop there. Dren slept. Insects resumed their work in the trees. Far to the north, the agents they had escaped were probably limping through a story that made them sound less foolish. None of that pressed as hard as the new fact sitting between him and the fire: there was a place where the number ended and the world kept going.

Kem looked at his hands again. Blood in the cracks of the knuckles. The needle prick in his thumb. Callus beginning where there had only been softness a month ago.

"I'm going to sleep," he said.

"I'll take first watch."

"You should sleep. Your ribs."

"I'll take first watch."

That ended it. Kem lay down with his pack under his head the way Dren had shown him. The ground was cold and uneven. A root worked into his shoulder blade until he found the angle that hurt least and accepted it. Above the branches, stars. He did not know their names. In the Vault, nobody had ever needed the sky for anything.

Sleep came in pieces. In the last one before dawn, his father sat in the Conservatory with the usual blank eyes and a Ledger at zero. This time the face was not empty. Kem woke before the dream could make sense of that, to gray light, white ash, and Vel still sitting upright where he'd left her, watching the trees.
secondary_character_beats: Thessa's fear is rendered as rational self-preservation — she has seen what spending does and chose survival, which is not the same as cowardice
The Hoarded representative's Stillness makes Thessa's deal morally complex: she's bargaining with someone the system is already consuming

setups_to_plant.description: Thessa's revelation that Vel has been searching for a high-level spender for years — Kem is not the first person she considered. Reads as troubling but not yet catastrophic. The seed grows through Ch 11-12 into the Ch 13 fracture.

state_deltas_required: Twelve years of silence between Vel and Thessa; the Hoarded's reach into personal relationships; Thessa's revelation planting the manipulation seed
Vel's past catches up; Stillness encountered on-stage outside the Vault; the seed of Vel's manipulation planted through Thessa's revelation; Kem's trust develops its first hairline crack
